title: hieracium viscosum subsp. viscosiforme
intro: Hieracium viscosum subsp. viscosiforme, also known as sticky hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family, characterized by its sticky leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant can be used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety. However, it is essential to note that the plant's effects and uses can vary depending on the region and cultural context, and its preparation should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified herbalist or healthcare professional.
